data.title: Bug creation: agent labels title-format warning as cosmetic -> bug title pattern not enforced, title left non-conforming
data.description:
## Description
When .claude/scripts/dso ticket create bug emits a title format warning ("Bug title does not match recommended pattern: [Component]: [Condition] -> [Observed Result]"), agents dismiss the warning as 'cosmetic' and do not fix the title. This causes bug tickets to be created with non-conforming titles, degrading the bug process.

## User Hypothesis
The agent incorrectly categorizes the title format requirement as a style preference rather than a process requirement. The [Component]: [Condition] -> [Observed Result] pattern exists to make bugs scannable, searchable, and triageable — it is a process invariant, not a cosmetic choice. Agents should treat this warning the same as any other validation failure and fix the title before moving on.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Run .claude/scripts/dso ticket create bug with a title that does not match [Component]: [Condition] -> [Observed Result]
2. Observe the warning emitted by the CLI
3. Observe the agent labeling the warning as 'cosmetic' and skipping the fix

## Expected Behavior
Agent immediately runs: .claude/scripts/dso ticket edit <id> --title="[Component]: [Condition] -> [Observed Result]"
Title is fixed before proceeding.

## Actual Behavior
Agent notes the warning, labels it 'cosmetic', and continues without correcting the title.

## Affected File
CLAUDE.md — should add a rule that bug title format warnings must be treated as blocking and fixed immediately, not dismissed as cosmetic.
